differential_diagnoses: Chronic portal vein thrombosis secondary to trauma-induced injury
Chronic mesenteric ischemia (abdominal angina) secondary to venous outflow obstruction
Occult malignancy (e.g., hepatobiliary or pancreatic) causing secondary venous obstruction
next_steps: Perform CT angiography of the abdomen to evaluate for mesenteric ischemia and patency of the mesenteric vessels
Perform percutaneous liver biopsy to evaluate for portal fibrosis versus cirrhosis
Perform transthoracic echocardiogram to rule out underlying cardiac contributions to congestive hepatopathy
Measurement of hepatic venous pressure gradient (HVPG)
Stool microscopy for ova and parasites
